But after no long time there beat down from it a tempestuous wind, which is called Euraquilo:
and when the ship was caught, and could not face the wind, we gave way to it, and were driven.
And running under the lee of a small island called Cauda, we were able, with difficulty, to secure the boat:
and when they had hoisted it up, they used helps, under-girding the ship; and, fearing lest they should be cast upon the Syrtis, they lowered the gear, and so were driven.
And as we labored exceedingly with the storm, the next day they began to throw the freight overboard;
and the third day they cast out with their own hands the tackling of the ship.
And when neither sun nor stars shone upon us for many days, and no small tempest lay on us, all hope that we should be saved was now taken away.

en.excerpt: When neither sun nor stars shone upon us for many days, and no small tempest lay on us, all hope that we should be saved was now taken away.

verses.text: 走的日子多了，已經過了禁食的節期，行船又危險，保羅就勸眾人說：
「眾位，我看這次行船，不但貨物和船要受傷損，大遭破壞，連我們的性命也難保。」
但百夫長信從掌船的和船主，不信從保羅所說的。
且因在這海口過冬不便，船上的人就多半說，不如開船離開這地方，或者能到非尼基過冬。非尼基是克里特的一個海口，一面朝東北，一面朝東南。
這時微微起了南風，他們以為得意，就起了錨，貼近克里特行去。
不多幾時，狂風從島上撲下來，那風名叫友拉革羅。
船被風抓住，敵不住風，我們就任風颳去。
貼著一個小島的背風岸奔行，那島名叫高大，在那裡僅僅收住了小船。
既然把小船拉上來，就用纜索捆綁船底，又恐怕在賽耳底沙灘上擱了淺，就落下篷來，任船飄去。
我們被風浪逼得甚急，第二天眾人就把貨物拋在海裡。
到第三天，他們又親手把船上的器具拋棄了。
太陽和星辰多日不顯露，又有狂風大浪催逼，我們得救的指望就都絕了。

zh.excerpt: 太陽和星辰多日不顯露，又有狂風大浪催逼，我們得救的指望就都絕了。

title.en: Paul's Angel Vision: 'Fear Not, You Must Stand Before Caesar'
title.zh: 保羅異見天使：「不要害怕！你必定站在愷撒面前」

description.en: After days without food Paul stands and rebukes the crew for not heeding his warning at Crete, then brings extraordinary encouragement: an angel of God appeared to him the previous night assuring him that he must stand before Caesar and that God has granted Paul the lives of all 276 aboard. Paul urges everyone to take heart, declaring his faith that it will happen exactly as God told him — though they will run aground on an island.
description.zh: 眾人多日未進食，保羅站起來責備他們當初不聽警告，隨即帶來奇妙的鼓勵：神的使者昨夜站在他旁邊，告訴他必定站在愷撒面前，且神已把全船276人的性命都賜給他。保羅勸眾人放心，宣告他信神所說的必然成就——雖然他們將要撞在一個島上。

verses.text: And when they had been long without food, then Paul stood forth in the midst of them, and said, Sirs, ye should have hearkened unto me, and not have set sail from Crete, and have gotten this injury and loss.
And now I exhort you to be of good cheer; for there shall be no loss of life among you, but only of the ship.
For there stood by me this night an angel of the God whose I am, whom also I serve,
saying, Fear not, Paul; thou must stand before Caesar: and lo, God hath granted thee all them that sail with thee.
Wherefore, sirs, be of good cheer: for I believe God, that it shall be even so as it hath been spoken unto me.
But we must be cast upon a certain island.

en.excerpt: Fear not, Paul; thou must stand before Caesar: and lo, God hath granted thee all them that sail with thee.

verses.text: 眾人多日沒有吃什麼，保羅就出來站在他們中間，說：「眾位，你們本該聽我的話不離開克里特，免得遭這樣的傷損破壞。
現在我還勸你們放心，你們的性命一個也不失喪，唯獨失喪這船。
因我所屬、所侍奉的神，他的使者昨夜站在我旁邊說：
「保羅，不要害怕！你必定站在愷撒面前；並且與你同船的人，神都賜給你了。」
所以眾位可以放心，我信神他怎樣對我說，事情也要怎樣成就。
只是我們必要撞在一個島上。」

zh.excerpt: 保羅，不要害怕！你必定站在愷撒面前；並且與你同船的人，神都賜給你了。

title.en: Sailors Try to Flee; Paul Stops Them
title.zh: 水手圖謀逃跑；保羅阻止他們

description.en: On the fourteenth night, drifting in the Adriatic, the sailors detect breakers and drop four anchors from the stern to wait for dawn. Under the pretext of laying bow anchors, they lower the lifeboat to abandon ship. Paul warns the centurion that unless the sailors remain, no one can be saved. The soldiers immediately cut the lifeboat's ropes and let it fall into the sea.
description.zh: 在亞得里亞海漂流的第十四夜，水手察覺正接近陸地，從船尾拋下四個錨等候天明。他們藉口要從船頭拋錨，偷偷放下小船意圖逃跑。保羅警告百夫長，若水手離船，眾人必不能得救。兵丁當即砍斷小船的繩索，讓它漂去。

verses.text: But when the fourteenth night was come, as we were driven to and fro in the sea of Adria, about midnight the sailors surmised that they were drawing near to some country:
and they sounded, and found twenty fathoms; and after a little space, they sounded again, and found fifteen fathoms.
And fearing lest haply we should be cast ashore on rocky ground, they let go four anchors from the stern, and wished for the day.
And as the sailors were seeking to flee out of the ship, and had lowered the boat into the sea, under color as though they would lay out anchors from the foreship,
Paul said to the centurion and to the soldiers, Except these abide in the ship, ye cannot be saved.
Then the soldiers cut away the ropes of the boat, and let her fall off.

en.excerpt: Except these abide in the ship, ye cannot be saved.

verses.text: 到了第十四天夜間，船在亞得里亞海飄來飄去，約到半夜，水手以為漸近旱地，
就探深淺，探得有十二丈；稍往前行，又探深淺，探得有九丈。
恐怕撞在石頭上，就從船尾拋下四個錨，盼望天亮。
水手想要逃出船去，把小船放在海裡，假作要從船頭拋錨的樣子。
保羅對百夫長和兵丁說：「這些人若不等在船上，你們必不能得救。」
於是兵丁砍斷小船的繩子，由它飄去。

zh.excerpt: 這些人若不等在船上，你們必不能得救。

title.en: Paul Breaks Bread and Encourages All 276 to Eat
title.zh: 保羅掰餅，勸勵全船276人進食

description.en: As dawn approaches after fourteen days of fasting, Paul urges everyone to eat for their survival, promising not a hair of their heads will be lost. He takes bread, gives thanks to God before all, breaks it, and begins to eat. The entire company of 276 is heartened and joins him. Afterward they throw the remaining wheat overboard to lighten the ship for the beach attempt.
description.zh: 天漸亮時，眾人已禁食十四天，保羅勸他們吃飯，應許他們一根頭髮也不會損壞。他在眾人面前拿餅、祝謝神、掰開吃。全船276人都放下心，一同進食。飯後，他們把剩餘的麥子拋進海裡，為使船輕便，好靠近岸邊。

verses.text: And while the day was coming on, Paul besought them all to take some food, saying, This day is the fourteenth day that ye wait and continue fasting, having taken nothing.
Wherefore I beseech you to take some food: for this is for your safety: for there shall not a hair perish from the head of any of you.
And when he had said this, and had taken bread, he gave thanks to God in the presence of all; and he brake it, and began to eat.
Then were they all of good cheer, and themselves also took food.
And we were in all in the ship two hundred threescore and sixteen souls.
And when they had eaten enough, they lightened the ship, throwing out the wheat into the sea.

en.excerpt: There shall not a hair perish from the head of any of you.

verses.text: 天漸亮的時候，保羅勸眾人都吃飯，說：「你們懸望忍餓不吃什麼，已經十四天了。
所以我勸你們吃飯，這是關乎你們救命的事，因為你們各人連一根頭髮也不至於損壞。」
保羅說了這話，就拿著餅，在眾人面前祝謝了神，掰開吃。
於是他們都放下心，也就吃了。
我們在船上的共有二百七十六個人。
他們吃飽了，就把船上的麥子拋在海裡，為要叫船輕一點。

zh.excerpt: 你們各人連一根頭髮也不至於損壞。

title.zh: 保羅遭遇海難

description.en: Paul survives a violent storm and shipwreck on the journey to Rome
description.zh: 保羅在前往羅馬的旅程中經歷暴風雨和海難後倖存

verses.text: And when it was day, they knew not the land: but they perceived a certain bay with a beach, and they took counsel whether they coulddrive the ship upon it.
And casting off the anchors, they left them in the sea, at the same time loosing the bands of the rudders; and hoisting up the foresail to the wind, they made for the beach.
But lighting upon a place where two seas met, they ran the vessel aground; and the foreship struck and remained unmoveable, but the stern began to break up by the violenceof the waves.
And the soldiers’ counsel was to kill the prisoners, lest anyof themshould swim out, and escape.

But the centurion, desiring to save Paul, stayed them from their purpose; and commanded that they who could swim should cast themselves overboard, and get first to the land; 44and the rest, some on planks, and some onotherthings from the ship. And so it came to pass, that they all escaped safe to the land.

en.excerpt: And when it was day, they knew not the land: but they perceived a certain bay...

verses.text: 到了天亮，他們不認識那地方，但見一個海灣，有岸可登，就商議能把船攏進去不能。
但遇著兩水夾流的地方，就把船擱了淺；船頭膠住不動，船尾被浪的猛力衝壞。
但百夫長要救保羅，不准他們任意而行，就吩咐會洑水的，跳下水去先上岸；
其餘的人可以用板子或船上的零碎東西上岸。這樣，眾人都得了救，上了岸。

zh.excerpt: 這樣，眾人都得了救，上了岸...

title.en: Paul Bitten by a Viper on Malta; Hailed as a God
title.zh: 保羅在馬耳他被毒蛇咬；土人稱他為神

description.en: The 276 survivors discover they are on an island called Malta. The hospitable islanders kindle a fire in the rain and cold. As Paul gathers wood, a viper driven out by the heat fastens on his hand. The islanders assume he is a murderer being punished by divine justice. But Paul shakes the snake off into the fire unharmed. When no swelling or death follows, they change their verdict: "He is a god."
description.zh: 276名生還者發現自己在一個名叫馬耳他的島上。好客的島民在雨中生火接待眾人。保羅拾柴時，一條被熱氣逼出的毒蛇咬住他的手。土人以為他是個凶手，正受天理懲罰。然而保羅把蛇甩進火裡，毫無傷害。他既不腫起，也未仆倒，土人便改口說：「他是個神！」

verses.text: And when we were escaped, then we knew that the island was called Melita.
And the barbarians showed us no common kindness: for they kindled a fire, and received us all, because of the present rain, and because of the cold.
But when Paul had gathered a bundle of sticks and laid them on the fire, a viper came out by reason of the heat, and fastened on his hand.

And when the barbarians saw the venomous creature hanging from his hand, they said one to another, No doubt this man is a murderer, whom, though he hath escaped from the sea, yet Justice hath not suffered to live.
Howbeit he shook off the creature into the fire, and took no harm.

But they expected that he would have swollen, or fallen down dead suddenly: but when they were long in expectation and beheld nothing amiss came to him, they changed their minds, and said that he was a god.

en.excerpt: They changed their minds, and said that he was a god.

verses.text: 我們既已得救，才知道那島名叫馬耳他。
土人看待我們有非常的情分，因為當時下雨，天氣又冷，就生火接待我們眾人。
那時，保羅拾起一捆柴，放在火上，有一條毒蛇因為熱了出來，咬住他的手。
土人看見那毒蛇懸在他手上，就彼此說：「這人必是個凶手，雖然從海裡救上來，天理還不容他活著。」
保羅竟把那毒蛇甩在火裡，並沒有受傷。
土人想他必要腫起來，或是忽然仆倒死了。看了多時，見他無害，就轉念說：「他是個神！」

zh.excerpt: 看了多時，見他無害，就轉念說：「他是個神！」

title.en: Paul Heals Publius's Father and Many on Malta
title.zh: 保羅醫治部百流之父及島上眾病人

description.en: The island's chief man Publius generously hosts the party for three days. Paul visits Publius's father, who is ill with fever and dysentery, prays, lays hands on him, and heals him. Word spreads and the rest of the sick on the island come and are healed. The grateful Maltese lavish honours on the group and supply everything needed when they sail after three months.
description.zh: 島長部百流熱情款待眾人三天。保羅探望部百流患熱病和痢疾的父親，禱告後按手在他身上，醫治了他。消息傳開，島上其餘的病人也來，全都得了醫治。感恩的馬耳他人大大尊敬他們，並在三個月後開船時，把眾人所需的物品一一送上船。

verses.text: Now in the neighborhood of that place were lands belonging to the chief man of the island, named Publius; who received us, and entertained us three days courteously.
And it was so, that the father of Publius lay sick of fever and dysentery: unto whom Paul entered in, and prayed, and laying his hands on him healed him.
And when this was done, the rest also that had diseases in the island came, and were cured:
who also honored us with many honors; and when we sailed, they put on board such things as we needed.

en.excerpt: Paul entered in, and prayed, and laying his hands on him healed him.

verses.text: 離那地方不遠，有田產是島長部百流的。他接納我們，盡情款待三日。
當時，部百流的父親患熱病和痢疾躺著。保羅進去為他禱告，按手在他身上，治好了他。
從此，島上其餘的病人也來，得了醫治。
他們又多方地尊敬我們，到了開船的時候，也把我們所需用的送到船上。

zh.excerpt: 保羅進去為他禱告，按手在他身上，治好了他。

title.en: Final Voyage from Malta to Rome; Brothers Come Out to Meet Paul
title.zh: 從馬耳他到羅馬的最後航程；弟兄們出城迎接保羅

description.en: After three months on Malta, they sail on an Alexandrian ship marked with the Twin Brothers (Castor and Pollux). They stop at Syracuse for three days, then Rhegium and Puteoli, where they find fellow believers and spend a week. As they approach Rome, brothers come out as far as the Market of Appius and the Three Taverns to meet them. Seeing them, Paul thanks God and takes courage. In Rome Paul is permitted to live in private lodgings with a single soldier guarding him.
description.zh: 在馬耳他停留三個月後，他們乘坐一艘以「宙斯雙子」為記的亞歷山大船繼續前行。在敘拉古停泊三天，再經利基翁、部丟利，在那裡遇見弟兄們，同住七天。接近羅馬時，弟兄們出城，一直來到亞比烏市和三館地方迎接。保羅見了他們，感謝神，放心壯膽。進入羅馬城後，保羅蒙准與看守他的一名兵丁另住一處。

verses.text: And after three months we set sail in a ship of Alexandria which had wintered in the island, whose sign was The Twin Brothers.
And touching at Syracuse, we tarried there three days.
And from thence we made a circuit, and arrived at Rhegium: and after one day a south wind sprang up, and on the second day we came to Puteoli;
where we found brethren, and were entreated to tarry with them seven days: and so we came to Rome.
And from thence the brethren, when they heard of us, came to meet us as far as The Market of Appius and The Three Taverns; whom when Paul saw, he thanked God, and took courage.
And when we entered into Rome, Paul was suffered to abide by himself with the soldier that guarded him.

en.excerpt: When Paul saw, he thanked God, and took courage.

verses.text: 過了三個月，我們上了亞歷山大的船往前行。這船以「宙斯雙子」為記，是在那海島過了冬的。
到了敘拉古，我們停泊三日，
又從那裡繞行，來到利基翁。過了一天，起了南風，第二天就來到部丟利。
在那裡遇見弟兄們，請我們與他們同住了七天。這樣，我們來到羅馬。
那裡的弟兄們一聽見我們的信息，就出來到亞比烏市和三館地方迎接我們。保羅見了他們，就感謝神，放心壯膽。
進了羅馬城，保羅蒙准和一個看守他的兵另住在一處。

zh.excerpt: 保羅見了他們，就感謝神，放心壯膽。

title.en: Paul Meets Rome's Jewish Leaders; Explains His Appeal
title.zh: 保羅會見羅馬猶太首領；解釋上訴的緣由

description.en: Three days after arriving, Paul summons Rome's Jewish leaders and explains his situation honestly: he is in chains through no fault against the Jewish people or customs, but because Jews in Jerusalem opposed his release, forcing him to appeal to Caesar — not to press charges against his own nation, but for the hope of Israel. The leaders say they have received no letters about him from Judea, but are curious about this sect, universally spoken against.
description.zh: 抵達三天後，保羅召集羅馬的猶太首領，坦誠說明自己的處境：他所戴的鎖鏈並非因為得罪了猶太百姓或祖宗的規條，而是因猶太人的反對，不得已上告於愷撒——目的並非控告本族，而是為以色列人的盼望。首領們說他們沒有從猶大接到關於保羅的信，但希望親耳聽聽他的見解，因為這教門到處被毀謗。

verses.text: And it came to pass, that after three days he called together those that were the chief of the Jews: and when they were come together, he said unto them, I, brethren, though I had done nothing against the people, or the customs of our fathers, yet was delivered prisoner from Jerusalem into the hands of the Romans:
who, when they had examined me, desired to set me at liberty, because there was no cause of death in me.
But when the Jews spake against it, I was constrained to appeal unto Caesar; not that I had aught whereof to accuse my nation.
For this cause therefore did I entreat you to see and to speak with me: for because of the hope of Israel I am bound with this chain.
And they said unto him, We neither received letters from Judaea concerning thee, nor did any of the brethren come hither and report or speak any harm of thee.
But we desire to hear of thee what thou thinkest: for as concerning this sect, it is known to us that everywhere it is spoken against.

en.excerpt: Because of the hope of Israel I am bound with this chain.

verses.text: 過了三天，保羅請猶太人的首領來。他們來了，就對他們說：「弟兄們，我雖沒有做什麼事干犯本國的百姓和我們祖宗的規條，卻被鎖綁，從耶路撒冷解在羅馬人的手裡。
他們審問了我，就願意釋放我，因為在我身上並沒有該死的罪。
無奈猶太人不服，我不得已，只好上告於愷撒，並非有什麼事要控告我本國的百姓。
因此，我請你們來見面說話。我原為以色列人所指望的，被這鏈子捆鎖。」
他們說：「我們並沒有接著從猶太來論你的信，也沒有弟兄到這裡來報給我們說你有什麼不好處。
但我們願意聽你的意見如何，因為這教門我們曉得是到處被毀謗的。」

zh.excerpt: 我原為以色列人所指望的，被這鏈子捆鎖。

title.en: Paul Expounds Scripture All Day; Quotes Isaiah's Warning to Unbelieving Jews
title.zh: 保羅整日講解聖經；以以賽亞書警告不信的猶太人

description.en: On an appointed day a large number of Jewish leaders come to Paul's lodging. From morning to evening he expounds the kingdom of God and persuades them about Jesus from Moses and the Prophets. The audience splits — some believe, some do not. As they leave in disagreement, Paul delivers a solemn final word: quoting Isaiah 6, he declares that the Holy Spirit spoke truly about their forefathers' hardened hearts, and announces that God's salvation is now sent to the Gentiles, who will listen.
description.zh: 在約定的日子，許多猶太首領來到保羅的寓所。保羅從早到晚，引摩西的律法和先知的書，證明神國的道，以耶穌的事勸勉他們。聽眾分裂——有人信，有人不信。他們議論紛紛地要散去時，保羅引用以賽亞書第六章作鄭重的最後宣告：聖靈藉以賽亞向他們祖宗所說的話是真實的，這百姓心蒙脂油，聽而不聞，看而不見；因此，神的救恩如今傳給外邦人，他們必要聽受。

verses.text: And when they had appointed him a day, they came to him into his lodging in great number; to whom he expounded the matter, testifying the kingdom of God, and persuading them concerning Jesus, both from the law of Moses and from the prophets, from morning till evening.
And some believed the things which were spoken, and some disbelieved.
And when they agreed not among themselves, they departed after that Paul had spoken one word, Well spake the Holy Spirit through Isaiah the prophet unto your fathers,
saying, Go thou unto this people, and say, By hearing ye shall hear, and shall in no wise understand; And seeing ye shall see, and shall in no wise perceive:

For this people's heart is waxed gross, And their ears are dull of hearing, And their eyes they have closed; Lest haply they should perceive with their eyes, And hear with their ears, And understand with their heart, And should turn again, And I should heal them.
Be it known therefore unto you, that this salvation of God is sent unto the Gentiles: they will also hear.
And when he had said these words, the Jews departed, having much disputing among themselves.

en.excerpt: This salvation of God is sent unto the Gentiles: they will also hear.

verses.text: 他們和保羅約定了日子，就有許多人到他的寓處來。保羅從早到晚，對他們講論這事，證明神國的道，引摩西的律法和先知的書，以耶穌的事勸勉他們。
他所說的話，有信的，有不信的。
他們彼此不合，就散了。未散以先，保羅說了一句話，說：「聖靈藉先知以賽亞向你們祖宗所說的話是不錯的。」
「他說：『你去告訴這百姓說：你們聽是要聽見，卻不明白；看是要看見，卻不曉得。」
「因為這百姓油蒙了心，耳朵發沉，眼睛閉著，恐怕眼睛看見，耳朵聽見，心裡明白，回轉過來，我就醫治他們。』」
「所以你們當知道，神這救恩如今傳給外邦人，他們也必聽受。」
保羅說了這話，猶太人議論紛紛地就走了。

zh.excerpt: 神這救恩如今傳給外邦人，他們也必聽受。

title.en: Paul's Two Years of Bold Ministry in Rome
title.zh: 保羅在羅馬放膽傳道足足兩年

description.en: Paul spends two full years in his own rented dwelling in Rome, welcoming all who come to him. With complete freedom to preach, he boldly proclaims the kingdom of God and teaches about the Lord Jesus Christ — and no one stops him. This is the triumphant closing scene of Acts: the gospel has reached the heart of the empire.
description.zh: 保羅在自己所租的房子裡住了足足兩年，凡來見他的人，他全都接待。他放膽傳講神國的道，將主耶穌基督的事教導人，並沒有人禁止。這是使徒行傳的最後一幕：福音已傳到帝國的心臟地帶。

verses.text: And he abode two whole years in his own hired dwelling, and received all that went in unto him,
preaching the kingdom of God, and teaching the things concerning the Lord Jesus Christ with all boldness, none forbidding him.

en.excerpt: Preaching the kingdom of God, and teaching the things concerning the Lord Jesus Christ with all boldness, none forbidding him.

verses.text: 保羅在自己所租的房子裡住了足足兩年。凡來見他的人，他全都接待，
放膽傳講神國的道，將主耶穌基督的事教導人，並沒有人禁止。

zh.excerpt: 放膽傳講神國的道，將主耶穌基督的事教導人，並沒有人禁止。

description.en: The holy city where Jesus was crucified and where the early church began after Pentecost
description.zh: 耶穌被釘十字架的聖城，五旬節後早期教會開始的地方

description.en: The mountain ridge east of Jerusalem where Jesus ascended to heaven
description.zh: 耶路撒冷東面的山脊，耶穌在此升天

description.en: An ancient city where Saul encountered Jesus on the road and was converted, becoming the Apostle Paul
description.zh: 古城，掃羅在前往此地的路上遇見耶穌並悔改，成為使徒保羅

description.en: The desert road where Philip met and baptized the Ethiopian eunuch
description.zh: 腓利遇見埃塞俄比亞太監並為他施洗的曠野之路

description.en: A Roman port city where Peter preached to Cornelius and the gospel first reached the Gentiles
description.zh: 羅馬港口城市，彼得向哥尼流傳道，福音首次傳給外邦人

description.en: Major city where believers were first called Christians and the base for Paul's missionary journeys
description.zh: 主要城市，信徒首次被稱為基督徒，保羅宣教旅程的基地

description.en: Roman colony in Macedonia where Paul established a church and the jailer was converted
description.zh: 馬其頓的羅馬殖民地，保羅建立教會，獄卒在此悔改

description.en: Cultural center of ancient Greece where Paul preached at the Areopagus about the unknown God
description.zh: 古希臘文化中心，保羅在亞略巴古講論未識之神

description.en: Wealthy commercial city where Paul established a strong church and met Aquila and Priscilla
description.zh: 富裕的商業城市，保羅建立堅固的教會並遇見亞居拉和百基拉

description.en: Major city in Asia Minor where a great revival occurred and magic books were burned
description.zh: 小亞細亞的主要城市，發生大復興並焚燒魔術書籍

description.en: Island where Paul and his companions were shipwrecked on the journey to Rome
description.zh: 保羅和同伴前往羅馬途中遭遇海難的島嶼

description.en: Capital of the Roman Empire where Paul was imprisoned and continued preaching the gospel
description.zh: 羅馬帝國首都，保羅被囚禁並繼續傳福音

description.en: A port city in northwest Asia Minor where Paul had visions and conducted missionary work, including the raising of Eutychus
description.zh: 小亞細亞西北部的港口城市，保羅在此有異象、從事宣教工作，並在此使猶推古復活

description.en: An ancient port city in Asia Minor where Paul gave his farewell address to the Ephesian elders on his final journey to Jerusalem
description.zh: 小亞細亞的古代港口城市，保羅在此向以弗所長老發表告別演說，然後踏上前往耶路撒冷的最後旅程

description.en: Port city of Antioch where Paul and Barnabas sailed from at the start of the first missionary journey (Acts 13:4)
description.zh: 安提阿的港口城市，保羅和巴拿巴在第一次宣教旅程開始時從這裡出航（使徒行傳13:4）

description.en: Port city on the eastern coast of Cyprus where Paul, Barnabas and John Mark first preached in the synagogues (Acts 13:5)
description.zh: 塞浦路斯東岸的港口城市，保羅、巴拿巴和約翰馬可在此首次在會堂傳道（使徒行傳13:5）

description.en: Capital of Cyprus where Paul blinded the sorcerer Elymas and the proconsul Sergius Paulus believed (Acts 13:6–12)
description.zh: 塞浦路斯首府，保羅在此使術士以呂馬失明，方伯士求信主（使徒行傳13:6–12）

description.en: City in Pamphylia where John Mark departed from Paul and Barnabas to return to Jerusalem (Acts 13:13; 14:25)
description.zh: 旁非利亞的城市，約翰馬可在此離開保羅和巴拿巴返回耶路撒冷（使徒行傳13:13；14:25）

description.en: City in Pisidia where Paul preached a landmark sermon in the synagogue and many Gentiles believed, though Jewish leaders expelled them (Acts 13:14–52)
description.zh: 彼西底的城市，保羅在會堂講道，許多外邦人信主，但猶太領袖驅逐了他們（使徒行傳13:14–52）

description.en: City where Paul and Barnabas preached and many believed, but a plot to stone them forced them to flee to Lystra and Derbe (Acts 14:1–7)
description.zh: 保羅和巴拿巴在此傳道，許多人信主，但有人密謀用石頭打他們，迫使他們逃往路司得和特庇（使徒行傳14:1–7）

description.en: City where Paul healed a lame man, the crowd mistook him for Hermes, and he was later stoned and left for dead; Timothy was from here (Acts 14:8–20)
description.zh: 保羅在此治癒一個瘸腿的人，群眾誤以為他是希耳米，後來他被石頭打昏倒地；提摩太就是這裡人（使徒行傳14:8–20）

description.en: City where Paul and Barnabas preached the gospel and made many disciples before beginning their return journey (Acts 14:20–21)
description.zh: 保羅和巴拿巴在此傳福音，得了許多門徒，然後開始返回的旅程（使徒行傳14:20–21）

modernName.zh: 土耳其南部與敘利亞北部

description.en: Region through which Paul and Silas traveled to strengthen the churches at the beginning of the second missionary journey (Acts 15:41)
description.zh: 保羅和西拉在第二次宣教旅程開始時經過這一帶，堅固眾教會（使徒行傳15:41）

description.en: Interior region where Paul and his companions were kept by the Holy Spirit from preaching in Asia (Acts 16:6)
description.zh: 保羅與同伴在這一帶被聖靈禁止在亞細亞講道（使徒行傳16:6）

description.en: Region where Paul's party attempted to go into Bithynia but was prevented by the Spirit (Acts 16:7)
description.zh: 保羅一行人在這裡想往庇推尼去，卻被聖靈攔阻（使徒行傳16:7）

description.en: Island stopover during the sea crossing from Troas to Macedonia (Acts 16:11)
description.zh: 從特羅亞前往馬其頓途中停靠的海島（使徒行傳16:11）

description.en: Port city where Paul and his companions landed in Macedonia (Acts 16:11)
description.zh: 保羅和同伴登陸馬其頓的港口城市（使徒行傳16:11）

description.en: Travel stop on the route from Philippi to Thessalonica (Acts 17:1)
description.zh: 從腓立比前往帖撒羅尼迦途中經過的停留地（使徒行傳17:1）

description.en: Another travel stop before reaching Thessalonica (Acts 17:1)
description.zh: 到達帖撒羅尼迦之前的另一個停留地（使徒行傳17:1）

description.en: Major Macedonian city where Paul preached in the synagogue and faced intense opposition (Acts 17:1-9)
description.zh: 馬其頓的重要城市，保羅在會堂講道，後來遭遇強烈反對（使徒行傳17:1-9）

description.en: City where many examined the Scriptures daily and believed Paul's message (Acts 17:10-14)
description.zh: 許多人天天考查聖經並相信保羅信息的城市（使徒行傳17:10-14）

description.en: Eastern port of Corinth where Paul cut his hair because of a vow before sailing (Acts 18:18)
description.zh: 哥林多東邊的港口，保羅因所許的願在此剪髮後啟航（使徒行傳18:18）

description.en: Region Paul passed through to strengthen disciples as he began the third missionary journey (Acts 18:23)
description.zh: 保羅開始第三次宣教旅程時經過此地，堅固門徒（使徒行傳18:23）

description.en: Region where Paul traveled encouraging believers during the third journey (Acts 20:1-3)
description.zh: 保羅在第三次旅程中經過此地，勸勉信徒（使徒行傳20:1-3）

description.en: Region where Paul spent three months, likely in Corinth, before returning north (Acts 20:2-3)
description.zh: 保羅在此停留三個月，很可能是在哥林多，之後再向北返回（使徒行傳20:2-3）

description.en: Port where Paul met his companions after traveling overland from Troas (Acts 20:13)
description.zh: 保羅從陸路離開特羅亞後，在此與同伴會合的港口（使徒行傳20:13）

modernName.zh: 希臘，萊斯博斯島米提利尼

description.en: Harbor stop on Paul's southbound voyage after leaving Assos (Acts 20:14)
description.zh: 保羅離開亞朔後南下航程中的停靠港（使徒行傳20:14）

description.en: Island stop during Paul's voyage toward Miletus (Acts 20:15)
description.zh: 保羅前往米利都途中停靠的海島（使徒行傳20:15）

description.en: Another island stop on the voyage down the coast of Asia Minor (Acts 20:15)
description.zh: 沿小亞細亞海岸南下航程中的另一個海島停靠點（使徒行傳20:15）

description.en: Island port where Paul's company stopped on the way toward Syria (Acts 21:1)
description.zh: 保羅一行前往敘利亞途中停靠的海島港口（使徒行傳21:1）

description.en: Island stop on the route from Cos toward Patara (Acts 21:1)
description.zh: 從哥士前往帕大喇途中停靠的海島（使徒行傳21:1）

description.en: Port where Paul transferred to a ship sailing for Phoenicia (Acts 21:1-2)
description.zh: 保羅在此轉乘前往腓尼基的船（使徒行傳21:1-2）

description.en: Phoenician port where Paul stayed with disciples who warned him about dangers ahead in Jerusalem (Acts 21:3-6)
description.zh: 腓尼基的港口，保羅與門徒同住，他們曾警告耶路撒冷的危險（使徒行傳21:3-6）

description.en: Port city where Paul greeted believers and stayed one day on the journey to Jerusalem (Acts 21:7)
description.zh: 保羅前往耶路撒冷途中問候信徒並住了一天的港口城市（使徒行傳21:7）

description.en: Port city in Pamphylia from which Paul and Barnabas sailed back to Antioch after completing the first missionary journey (Acts 14:25)
description.zh: 旁非利亞的港口城市，保羅和巴拿巴在完成第一次宣教旅程後從這裡乘船返回安提阿（使徒行傳14:25）

description.en: The central figure of Christianity, whose teachings and resurrection form the foundation of the Christian faith.
description.zh: 基督教的中心人物，其教導和復活構成了基督教信仰的基礎。

description.en: Originally a fisherman, Peter became one of Jesus' closest disciples and a leader of the early church. He preached the first sermon at Pentecost and was instrumental in spreading the gospel to both Jews and Gentiles.
description.zh: 彼得原本是一名漁夫，成為耶穌最親密的門徒之一和早期教會的領袖。他在五旬節講了第一篇道，在向猶太人和外邦人傳福音方面發揮了重要作用。

description.en: One of the twelve apostles, known as the disciple whom Jesus loved. He was present at key moments and later wrote the Gospel of John.
description.zh: 十二使徒之一，被稱為耶穌所愛的門徒。他在關鍵時刻在場，後來寫了約翰福音。

description.en: Originally a persecutor of Christians, Paul had a dramatic conversion on the road to Damascus. He became the most influential missionary of the early church, writing much of the New Testament and establishing churches throughout the Roman Empire.
description.zh: 保羅原本是基督徒的迫害者，在大馬士革的路上經歷了戲劇性的悔改。他成為早期教會最有影響力的宣教士，撰寫了大部分新約聖經，並在羅馬帝國各地建立教會。

description.en: One of the seven deacons chosen to serve the early church, Stephen was full of faith and power. He performed great wonders and was the first Christian martyr, stoned to death for his bold testimony.
description.zh: 司提反是被選出來服事早期教會的七位執事之一，滿有信心和能力。他行了大神蹟，是第一位基督教殉道者，因其大膽的見證而被石頭打死。

description.en: One of the seven deacons, Philip was a powerful evangelist who preached in Samaria and led the Ethiopian eunuch to faith in Christ. His ministry demonstrated the gospel breaking cultural barriers.
description.zh: 腓利是七位執事之一，是一位強有力的傳道者，他在撒馬利亞傳道，並帶領埃塞俄比亞太監信主。他的事工展示了福音打破文化障礙。

description.en: Known as 'Son of Encouragement', Barnabas was a generous supporter of the early church who introduced Paul to the apostles and traveled with him on missionary journeys, demonstrating grace and encouragement.
description.zh: 被稱為「勸慰子」的巴拿巴是早期教會的慷慨支持者，他將保羅介紹給使徒們，並與他一起進行宣教旅程，展示了恩典和鼓勵。

description.en: A physician and companion of Paul, Luke wrote the Gospel of Luke and the Book of Acts. His detailed historical accounts provide much of what we know about the early church and Jesus' ministry.
description.zh: 路加是一位醫生，也是保羅的同伴，他寫了路加福音和使徒行傳。他詳細的歷史記載為我們提供了許多關於早期教會和耶穌事工的知識。

role.en: Mother of Jesus and prayerful witness
role.zh: 耶穌的母親與禱告中的見證人

description.en: Present with the apostles and other believers in prayer after Jesus' ascension. Her appearance in Acts links the story of Jesus' birth to the birth of the church.
description.zh: 耶穌升天後，她與使徒和其他信徒一同恆切禱告。她在使徒行傳中的出現，把耶穌降生的故事連到教會誕生的時刻。

name.zh: 約翰馬可的母親馬利亞

role.en: Jerusalem believer and house church host
role.zh: 耶路撒冷信徒與家庭教會主人

description.en: A Jerusalem believer whose house became a gathering place for prayer. After Peter's release from prison, he went to her home where many were assembled.
description.zh: 她是耶路撒冷的信徒，她的家成為眾人聚集禱告的地方。彼得出獄後來到她家時，已有許多人在那裡同心祈禱。

description.en: The servant girl who recognized Peter's voice at the gate after his miraculous release from prison and ran to tell the believers.
description.zh: 彼得奇蹟出獄後，她在門口聽出彼得的聲音，便跑去告訴屋內的信徒。

description.en: A disciple in Joppa known for good works and care for the poor. Peter raised her from the dead, and many in the city turned to the Lord.
description.zh: 她是住在約帕的女門徒，滿有善行與賙濟。彼得使她從死裡復活，叫城中許多人因此歸向主。

description.en: She conspired with her husband to misrepresent the proceeds of a land sale. When confronted by Peter, she also fell under God's judgment.
description.zh: 她與丈夫同謀，謊報賣地所得。當彼得質問她時，她也落在神的審判之下。

description.en: One of the Athenians who believed after Paul's sermon at the Areopagus, showing that the gospel reached even the city's intellectual circles.
description.zh: 她是保羅在亞略巴古講道後信主的雅典人之一，顯示福音也進入了城中的思想與辯論圈。

role.zh: 商人與腓立比的接待者

description.en: A seller of purple cloth from Thyatira whose heart the Lord opened in Philippi. Her household became an early base of support for the church there.
description.zh: 她來自推雅推喇，販賣紫色布匹；主在腓立比開啟她的心。她的家後來成為當地教會早期的重要接待與聚集之處。

description.en: A Jewish believer who served alongside Aquila. She welcomed Paul, traveled in ministry, and helped explain the way of God more accurately to Apollos.
description.zh: 她是與亞居拉一同事奉的猶太信徒。她接待保羅、在宣教旅程中同行，並向亞波羅更詳細地講解神的道。

description.en: The wife of Governor Felix who heard Paul speak about faith in Christ Jesus, righteousness, self-control, and coming judgment.
description.zh: 她是巡撫腓力斯的猶太妻子，曾與丈夫一同聽保羅講論信基督耶穌之道、公義、節制和將來的審判。

description.en: The sister of King Agrippa II who was present in Caesarea when Festus explained Paul's case and when Paul gave his formal defense before the royal court.
description.zh: 她是亞基帕二世的妹妹，在該撒利亞陪同王室聽非斯都陳述保羅的案件，也在保羅正式申辯時在場。

description.en: Grandson of Herod the Great, Agrippa I ruled as king over Judea. He executed the apostle James and imprisoned Peter, seeking to please the Jewish leaders. He was struck dead by an angel when he accepted divine worship (Acts 12).
description.zh: 大希律之孫，亞基帕一世統治猶太地。他殺害使徒雅各，並囚禁彼得，以取悅猶太領袖。當他接受百姓的神化崇拜時，被主的使者擊打而死（使徒行傳第12章）。

description.en: The high priest who presided over the Sanhedrin council during Paul's trials in Jerusalem. He ordered Paul to be struck on the mouth and later conspired with the chief priests and elders to have Paul ambushed (Acts 23–24).
description.zh: 在保羅於耶路撒冷受審期間主持公會的大祭司。他命人打保羅的嘴，後來又與祭司長和長老密謀伏擊保羅（使徒行傳第23–24章）。

description.en: The Roman governor who heard Paul's case in Caesarea. Though he found no guilt in Paul, he kept him imprisoned for two years, hoping for a bribe. He trembled when Paul reasoned about righteousness and self-control but ultimately dismissed him (Acts 24).
description.zh: 在該撒利亞審訊保羅的羅馬長官。雖然他找不到保羅有罪，卻將他關押兩年，期望得到賄賂。當保羅講論公義和節制時，他雖心中恐懼，卻最終將他打發走（使徒行傳第24章）。

description.en: Felix's successor as Roman governor. He inherited Paul's case and, after listening to his defense, declared him mad for believing in the resurrection. Seeking to do the Jews a favor, he asked Paul if he would go to Jerusalem for trial — prompting Paul's appeal to Caesar (Acts 25–26).
description.zh: 腓力斯的繼任者，接手保羅的案件。聽完保羅的申辯後，他宣稱保羅因相信復活而發狂。為了討好猶太人，他詢問保羅是否願意去耶路撒冷受審，此舉促使保羅上訴於凱撒（使徒行傳第25–26章）。

description.en: Son of Herod Agrippa I, he heard Paul's eloquent defense before Festus. Paul's appeal nearly persuaded him: 'In a short time you think to make me a Christian!' He agreed Paul was innocent but could not release him since Paul had appealed to Caesar (Acts 26).
description.zh: 希律亞基帕一世之子，在非斯都面前聆聽保羅的申辯。保羅幾乎說服了他：「你想用這點時候便叫我做基督徒呢！」他同意保羅無罪，但因保羅已上訴於愷撒而無法釋放他（使徒行傳第26章）。

description.en: A silversmith in Ephesus who made silver shrines of Artemis. Fearing that Paul's preaching would destroy his trade, he incited a riot in the city theatre that drew a crowd of thousands chanting 'Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!' (Acts 19).
description.zh: 以弗所的銀匠，製造亞底米神龕為業。擔心保羅的傳道會毀掉他的生意，他煽動暴亂，引發城中數千人湧入戲園，高喊「以弗所人的亞底米女神是大的！」（使徒行傳第19章）。

description.en: A Jewish sorcerer and false prophet on Cyprus who opposed Paul and Barnabas before the proconsul Sergius Paulus. Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, rebuked him and struck him temporarily blind — which led the proconsul to believe (Acts 13).
description.zh: 塞浦路斯的猶太術士和假先知，在方伯士求保羅面前阻擋保羅和巴拿巴。保羅充滿聖靈，斥責他並使他暫時失明——此事導致方伯士求信主（使徒行傳第13章）。

keyEvent.en: The Holy Spirit sets apart Barnabas and Saul for missionary work; the church fasts, prays, and sends them out.
keyEvent.zh: 聖靈揀選巴拿巴和掃羅進行宣教工作；教會禁食禱告，差派他們出去。

keyEvent.en: Paul and Barnabas set sail from the port of Seleucia, beginning their first missionary journey.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅和巴拿巴從西流基港出發，開始第一次宣教旅程。

keyEvent.en: They preach in the synagogues of Salamis. John Mark accompanies them as a helper.
keyEvent.zh: 他們在撒拉米的會堂傳道，約翰馬可陪同他們作助手。

keyEvent.en: Paul blinds the sorcerer Elymas (Bar-Jesus). The proconsul Sergius Paulus believes after witnessing the miracle.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅使術士以呂馬（巴耶穌）失明，方伯士求見證神蹟後信主。

keyEvent.en: They arrive at Perga in Pamphylia. John Mark departs and returns to Jerusalem.
keyEvent.zh: 他們抵達旁非利亞的別加，約翰馬可離隊返回耶路撒冷。

keyEvent.en: Paul preaches a landmark sermon in the synagogue. Many Gentiles believe, but Jewish leaders oppose them and drive them out of the region.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅在會堂講道，許多外邦人信主，但猶太領袖反對並將他們趕出境。

keyEvent.en: Many Jews and Gentiles believe. Opposition grows, and a plot to stone Paul and Barnabas forces them to flee to Lystra and Derbe.
keyEvent.zh: 許多猶太人和外邦人信主，但反對加劇，謀石殺意迫使保羅和巴拿巴逃往路司得和特庇。

keyEvent.en: Paul heals a man lame from birth. The crowd hails Barnabas as Zeus and Paul as Hermes. Paul is later stoned and left for dead.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅治癒生來瘸腿之人，群眾稱巴拿巴為宙斯、保羅為希耳米，保羅後來被石頭打倒昏死。

keyEvent.en: Paul and Barnabas preach the gospel in Derbe and make many disciples. The journey turns back.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅和巴拿巴在特庇傳福音，得許多門徒，然後折回。

keyEvent.en: They return to Lystra to strengthen and encourage the disciples.
keyEvent.zh: 他們返回路司得，堅固鼓勵門徒。

keyEvent.en: They return to Iconium to encourage believers and appoint elders in the church.
keyEvent.zh: 他們返回以哥念，勸勉信徒並設立長老。

keyEvent.en: They return to Pisidian Antioch, continuing to strengthen churches and appoint elders.
keyEvent.zh: 他們返回彼西底的安提阿，繼續堅固教會並設立長老。

keyEvent.en: Passing through Pisidia and Pamphylia, they preach the word in Perga on their return.
keyEvent.zh: 途經彼西底和旁非利亞，在別加傳道後繼續返程。

keyEvent.en: They travel to Attalia, the port city, to sail back to Syria.
keyEvent.zh: 他們前往港口城市亞大利，從那裡乘船返回敘利亞。

keyEvent.en: They return to Antioch and report to the church all that God had done among the Gentiles through them.
keyEvent.zh: 他們返回安提阿，向教會報告神藉著他們為外邦人所成就的一切。

keyEvent.en: Paul proposes revisiting the churches founded on the first missionary journey.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅提議重訪第一次宣教旅程所建立的眾教會。

keyEvent.en: Paul and Silas travel through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅和西拉走遍敘利亞和基利家，堅固眾教會。

keyEvent.en: Paul begins revisiting the churches planted during the first journey.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅開始重訪第一次旅程所建立的教會。

keyEvent.en: Timothy joins Paul and becomes part of the missionary team.
keyEvent.zh: 提摩太加入保羅，成為宣教團隊的一員。

keyEvent.en: The Holy Spirit prevents them from preaching in the province of Asia.
keyEvent.zh: 聖靈禁止他們在亞細亞省講道。

keyEvent.en: They attempt to enter Bithynia, but the Spirit does not permit them.
keyEvent.zh: 他們想往庇推尼去，卻被聖靈攔阻。

keyEvent.en: Paul receives the Macedonian vision calling the team into Europe; Luke likely joins here.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅領受馬其頓的異象，呼召他們前往歐洲；路加很可能在此加入。

keyEvent.en: The team stops at Samothrace during the voyage across the Aegean Sea.
keyEvent.zh: 團隊橫渡愛琴海時在撒摩特喇停靠。

keyEvent.en: They land at the Macedonian port city of Neapolis.
keyEvent.zh: 他們在馬其頓的港口城市尼亞波利登陸。

keyEvent.en: Lydia is converted, a slave girl is delivered, Paul and Silas are imprisoned, and the Philippian jailer believes.
keyEvent.zh: 呂底亞信主，使女得釋放，保羅和西拉被囚，腓立比禁卒歸信。

keyEvent.en: Amphipolis serves as a travel stop on the road toward Thessalonica.
keyEvent.zh: 暗妃波里是前往帖撒羅尼迦途中的停留站。

keyEvent.en: They pass through Apollonia as another stop on the route westward.
keyEvent.zh: 他們又經過亞波羅尼亞，繼續向西前行。

keyEvent.en: Paul preaches in the synagogue; some believe, but persecution forces the team to leave.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅在會堂講道，有些人信主，但逼迫興起，迫使團隊離開。

keyEvent.en: The Bereans examine the Scriptures daily and many believe.
keyEvent.zh: 庇哩亞人天天考查聖經，許多人因此相信。

keyEvent.en: Paul delivers the Areopagus sermon about the unknown God.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅在亞略巴古發表關於未識之神的講道。

keyEvent.en: Paul stays about eighteen months, works with Aquila and Priscilla, and stands before Gallio.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅停留約十八個月，與亞居拉和百基拉同工，並在迦流面前受審。

keyEvent.en: At the port of Cenchrea, Paul cuts his hair because of a vow before sailing.
keyEvent.zh: 在堅革哩港口，保羅因所許的願剪髮後啟航。

keyEvent.en: Paul reasons briefly in the synagogue at Ephesus and promises to return.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅在以弗所會堂短暫講論，並答應將來再回來。

keyEvent.en: The team arrives back in Judea by way of Caesarea.
keyEvent.zh: 團隊經由該撒利亞返回猶太地。

keyEvent.en: Paul goes up to greet the church in Jerusalem.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅上耶路撒冷去問候教會。

keyEvent.en: Paul returns to Antioch, bringing the second missionary journey to a close.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅回到安提阿，第二次宣教旅程至此結束。

keyEvent.en: Paul departs again from Antioch after spending some time there.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅在安提阿停留一段時間後再次出發。

keyEvent.en: Paul travels through Galatia and Phrygia, strengthening the disciples in the churches founded earlier.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅走遍加拉太和弗呂家，堅固先前建立的教會中的門徒。

keyEvent.en: Ephesus becomes Paul's major ministry center for about three years, marked by miracles, transformed lives, and the riot led by Demetrius.
keyEvent.zh: 以弗所成為保羅約三年的主要事工中心，伴隨神蹟、生命改變，以及底米丟帶起的暴動。

keyEvent.en: Paul travels through Macedonia encouraging believers, likely including churches in Philippi, Thessalonica, and Berea.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅走遍馬其頓，勸勉信徒，很可能包括腓立比、帖撒羅尼迦和庇哩亞的教會。

keyEvent.en: Paul spends three months in Greece, probably centered in Corinth.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅在希臘停留三個月，很可能以哥林多為中心。

keyEvent.en: Because of a plot by some Jews, Paul returns north through Macedonia instead of sailing directly from Greece.
keyEvent.zh: 由於猶太人的計謀，保羅沒有直接從希臘起航，而是改道經馬其頓北返。

keyEvent.en: After the Feast of Unleavened Bread, Paul sails from Philippi.
keyEvent.zh: 除酵節後，保羅從腓立比啟航。

keyEvent.en: At Troas, Paul raises Eutychus after he falls from the window during a long sermon.
keyEvent.zh: 在特羅亞，保羅長時間講道時，以推古從窗台跌下，後被保羅使其復活。

keyEvent.en: Paul goes overland to Assos while his companions travel by ship.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅走陸路到亞朔，而同伴乘船前往。

keyEvent.en: Mitylene is a stop on the southbound sea voyage.
keyEvent.zh: 米推利尼是南下航程中的停靠站。

keyEvent.en: The company continues south, stopping off Chios.
keyEvent.zh: 同工團隊繼續南下，在希阿附近停靠。

keyEvent.en: Samos serves as another sailing stop on the route toward Miletus.
keyEvent.zh: 撒摩是前往米利都途中另一個航海停靠站。

keyEvent.en: Paul summons the Ephesian elders to Miletus and delivers his moving farewell address.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅在米利都召來以弗所長老，發表感人的告別講話。

keyEvent.en: Cos is the next stop as the voyage turns toward Syria.
keyEvent.zh: 航程轉向敘利亞時，下一站是哥士。

keyEvent.en: The ship stops at Rhodes on the way east.
keyEvent.zh: 船隻東行途中停靠羅底。

keyEvent.en: At Patara, Paul transfers to a ship bound for Phoenicia.
keyEvent.zh: 在帕大喇，保羅轉乘前往腓尼基的船。

keyEvent.en: Paul stays with disciples in Tyre, who warn him about dangers awaiting him in Jerusalem.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅在推羅與門徒同住，他們警告他耶路撒冷將有危險。

keyEvent.en: At Ptolemais, Paul greets the believers and stays one day.
keyEvent.zh: 在多利買，保羅問候信徒並停留一天。

keyEvent.en: Paul stays with Philip the evangelist in Caesarea, where Agabus warns of the imprisonment ahead.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅在該撒利亞住在傳福音的腓利家中，亞迦布預言他將被捆綁。

keyEvent.en: Paul arrives in Jerusalem and is welcomed by believers, setting the stage for his arrest.
keyEvent.zh: 保羅抵達耶路撒冷，信徒歡迎他，接著展開後續被捕的事件。
